name: simple
description: Grading rules for 1-2 step tasks where correctness matters more than tool variety or long execution traces.
philosophy: The simple ruleset keeps the default focus on execution quality, but lowers the ceiling for short valid tasks such as timers, memory writes, or single-query lookups. It rewards one useful tool call and one real step instead of requiring multi-tool, multi-step flows.

signals.name: tool_diversity
signals.reason: +1 used at least one tool ({actual} distinct tools)

signals.name: routing_penalty
signals.reason: -1 at least one step had zero tools available

signals.name: suspicious_short_penalty
signals.reason: -1 task flagged as suspiciously short by runtime

signals.name: empty_answer_success_penalty
signals.reason: -1 task reported success but produced no answer

signals.name: ref_mismatch_penalty
signals.reason: -1 clicked {actual} element refs not in snapshot

signals.name: task_failed_penalty
signals.reason: -2 task outcome is an explicit failure (llm_error/failed/early_abort)

golden_requirements.reason: GOLDEN requires no tool repeated 3+ times consecutively
GOLDEN requires a non-empty final answer when reporting success
